bernadette-fong.name: Bernadette Fong
bernadette-fong.writeup:
Hi! I'm Bernadette and I completed my undergraduate in Mechanical Engineering with a Robotics specialisation and a minor in Life Sciences. I'm currently studying Financial Engineering at the Risk Management Institute.

I like learning and took as many interesting as courses I could handle, including convex optimisation, business law, and business econometrics. Beyond that, I've also completed the Associate Data Scientist in Python pathway on DataCamp and the Foundations in AI course by AISG.

My internship and final year project has let me explore some of the areas where Life Science and Engineering intersect, two of the things that have fascinated me for a long time.

Outside of academics, I love hiking and travelling, gymnastics/calisthenics, and rock climbing/bouldering. Some mountains I've summmitted include Scout Peak (5710m) in the Himalayas, Mytikas Peak of Mount Olympus (2918m), and Mount Rinjani in Lombok (3726m).

However, my personal mountain to climb is finding in a career that gives me such a strong sense of purpose that I would want to continue in it even when I feel secure in my finances.

ian-ng.name: Ian Ng
ian-ng.writeup:
Hello! My name is Ian and I am a graduate student pursuing a MSc in Data Science. Previously, I majored in Computer Engineering for my undergraduate degree. I have enjoyed my time in E-Scholars very much and am thankful for the friends I have made along the way.

I am a strong believer in the pay-it-forward culture and will gladly offer my advice/mentorship to any junior regardless of major. Feel free to drop me a message if you require any help from me, and I will be more than happy to assist you in any way I can!

ryan-sui.name: Ryan Sui
ryan-sui.writeup: Hi everyone, I am Ryan Sui, a Y4 E-Scholar having completed my undergraduate studies in Materials Science in Engineering (MSE) and am currently enrolled in the Masters in Business Analytics (MSBA) programme in NUS. My academic interests involved attempting to apply data-driven analytical techniques to different areas of material science such as cheminformatics. Feel free to reach out and ask any questions you may have, academic or otherwise, regarding the programme and student life at NUS in general. See you guys around!
ryan-sui.notable_achievements: Surviving e-scholars (barely; remains to be truly seen).

yim-hau-yin-ethan.name: Yim Hau Yin, Ethan
yim-hau-yin-ethan.writeup:
Hi! I'm Ethan and I major in Mechanical Engineering, with a specialisation in Aeronautical Engineering. I have a keen interest in the aviation industry and aspire to be a pilot after I graduate. In my free time, I enjoy swimming, listening to music and shooting the breeze with my friends.
Feel free to chat with me - I love getting to know new people!
yim-hau-yin-ethan.notable_achievements:
O-Level German
RC4 Swimming IG Captain
Outstanding Student Award

matthew-yip.name: Matthew Yip
matthew-yip.writeup: Hello! I am Matthew Yip, a MSc. Computer Engineering student having previously studied Mechanical Engineering for my undergraduate degree with a Second Major in Computer Science and a Minor in Innovation and Design. I have a deep passion for both engineering and tech, and this reflects in my hobbies that include 3D Printing and coding with Raspberry Pis!
matthew-yip.notable_achievements:
NOC Munich AY 23/24 Sem 2
Hack and Roll 2025 Most Overengineered Award (Toaster-Chan)

xue-tingkai.name: Xue Tingkai
xue-tingkai.notable_achievements:
Made audience laugh for 10s using a single line
Able to derive Navier-Stokes equation
Won my friend at scissors, paper, stone

isaac-poh-wen-kang.name: Isaac Poh Wen Kang
isaac-poh-wen-kang.writeup: I'm Isaac, Y3 in Mechanical Engineering, with a passion for the Aerospace engineering and playing music.

lim-heng-wee-ivan.name: Lim Heng Wee Ivan
lim-heng-wee-ivan.writeup: I'm part of the E-scholars Programme and an aspiring aerospace engineer. I'm passionate about making a difference in the aerospace industry. I am deeply interested in the field of biomimicry in flight mechanics.
lim-heng-wee-ivan.interests_hobbies:
Reading both non-fiction books on technology and fiction novels
Snowboarding

ellia-tio.name: Ellia Tio
ellia-tio.writeup: Hello! I’m Ellia and I’m a Year 3 Electrical Engineering student. I’m planning to finish an IOT specialization and a minor in Information Security.

tan-hsien-rong.name: Tan Hsien Rong
tan-hsien-rong.writeup:
Hi there! I’m Hsien Rong and I am a Year 3 Electrical Engineering undergraduate under the E-Scholars Programme :)

Engineering is my passion. During my pre-university days, my avid participation in robotics competitions and hackathons led me to discover my love for building and designing things. Today, as an electrical engineering undergraduate, I continue to pursue my passion in robotics and programming. I remain captivated by the endless possibilities of what I can create, and am always excited to see my own creations come to life.

Outside of NUS, I've worked with microcontroller programming, IoT, computer vision, signal processing and antenna design during internships with DSO and DSTA. I've also dabbled in app and game programming (Android Studio, Unity Engine, Godot Engine) for my personal projects.

I am excited to be part of the E-Scholars Programme, and I look forward to continue making new friends and learning new things as I enter my final year as an undergrad!

goh-ron-jay.name: Goh Ron-Jay
goh-ron-jay.writeup:
Hi I'm Jay, I study EE (and MSE). I strongly believe in passing it forward so I do a bunch of outreach stuff for escholars and CDE. I went to UIUC for exchange and that was a life-changing experience, highly recommend going for exchange.
Professionally I hope to work in semicon or signal processing, hopefully in the US maybe. Personally, I hope to retire as a tour guide ^_^.
goh-ron-jay.notable_achievements:
Fell asleep at airport gate and watched my plane fly away
completing a 6-day 100km hike + camp along the Australian coast

alyxia-seah.name: Alyxia Seah
alyxia-seah.writeup:
Hi! I'm Alyxia, and I'm a Year 3 student majoring in Biomedical Engineering and Mathematics. I'm passionate about using math and computation to solve problems in biochemistry and biology, and I intend to work in mathematical or scientific research after graduation. Some of my academic interests include applied math and computational biology, specifically protein folding!

In my free time, I love solving and creating all kinds of puzzles. I participate in puzzle hunts regularly and I'm also part of Solve@NUS, an NUS interest group for avid puzzlers. If you enjoy solving any kinds of mind-bending puzzles, like word puzzles, logic puzzles, and more, please join us!

Feel free to reach out to me if you'd like to chat about anything!
alyxia-seah.notable_achievements:
Biomedical Engineering Achievement Award (2023)
Champions in League of Legends Inter-College Games (2023)

claire-lam.name: Claire Lam
claire-lam.writeup:
Hi! My name is Claire and I am a Year 3 undergraduate majoring in Biomedical Engineering, with a specialization in Biomedical Materials and a Minor in Public Health.
A personal motto that I try to live by is “Not to make a living for myself, but to make living better for others.” This is one of the reasons I chose to major in Engineering, specifically Biomedical Engineering, as I aspire to innovate to improve lives.
The e-scholar community has been so supportive, fun and uplifting and I'm incredibly thankful for the friendships I've forged here so far! I'm definitely looking forward to the future memories we'll make together! 😁
claire-lam.notable_achievements:
Vice Captain, ACSI Cross Country A’Division 20/21,
Interaction Committee for Connecting MINDS (student-initiated project supporting the Movement for the Intellectually Disabled of Singapore) (2020),
Events Director (Welfare), E-Scholars Student Committee AY22/23
claire-lam.interests_hobbies:
Outdoor activities like hiking/running/just walks
Watching kdramas and animes
I love finding new music and going karaoke!

kenneth-goh.name: Kenneth Goh
kenneth-goh.writeup:
Hello everyone! My name is Kenneth, a year 3 Biomedical Engineering student with a specialization in Tissue Engineering, and a Minor in Life Science.

When I am free, you will see me at RC4 Squash and Spikeball IG sessions normally or just random sports IG.

Reach out to me if you have any questions, or would just like to have someone to talk to :D

ng-yu-fei.name: Ng Yu Fei
ng-yu-fei.writeup:
Hi! My name is Yu Fei and I'm an E-scholar majoring in Biomedical Engineering with a minor in Life Sciences. Despite not taking physics in A-levels, my keen passion in Biology and Design encouraged me to explore the field of Engineering.

In my past industry experience, I had the privilege of being involved in the design and processing of 3D prints using CAD software. I primarily worked with SLA technology (Formlabs, Asiga), with brief experience handling SLS and FDM prints as well. I was also fortunate enough to be offered a role as a Global Markets Intern, where I got to expose myself to the market dynamics while working on the trading floor.

In my free time, I enjoy gaming and taking naps. I especially enjoy playing sports or just being outdoors in general. Outside of E-scholars and RC4, I am also involved in Chinese Orchestra and Muay Thai CCAs.

I am constantly seeking for new opportunities to learn and gain new insights from others, or to share some experiences of my own. If you are considering pursuing Engineering without a Physics background, or have an interest in macrobiology/ enotomology, feel free to connect with me :-)
ng-yu-fei.interests_hobbies:
Sports
Nature & Hiking
Looking at ants

clive-choong.name: Clive Choong
clive-choong.writeup:
Hi everyone! I'm a final year undergraduate studying Biomedical Engineering with a minor in Public Health. I'm extremely passionate about healthcare and medical technology, and I've worked on projects and internships in the areas of Biorobotics, Biomaterials, Rehabilitation Medicine, Bioinstrumentation and Mental Health. During my  semester abroad at Johns Hopkins, I completed “Identity Formation for Future Healthcare Professionals” under Dr Margaret Chisolm, learning about the foundation required to thrive as a physician and I am currently reading a Pre-Med class at Duke NUS headed by Dr Wong Peiyan.

Outside of school, I volunteer as a research consultant for the Ministry of Education to train secondary and JC students for the International Science and Engineering Fair and the Singapore Science and Engineering Fair. During my free time, I go cycling in the evenings after school and bake on the weekends!

Feel free to reach out if you have any similar interests or would just like to connect :)

teo-jia-xin.name: Teo Jia Xin
teo-jia-xin.writeup:
Hello! I'm Jia Xin, a final year student studying Environmental Engineering and Economics (Double Degree Programme) :) 

I am really passionate about learning new things, as it makes me realise that there is so much in the world that I can discover more about. Outside of academics, I have had many experiences, ranging from leadership positions, volunteering, to internships in different disciplines. 

Through the experiences I have gone through and the skills that I have picked up, my greater goal is to contribute back to the community. In the future, I hope to work in policy or sustainability-related roles, to improve the lives of people through the work that I do, and ensure that Singapore can grow sustainably with inclusivity in mind.

Feel free to connect with me to discuss anything ranging from current affairs, sustainability, economics, or anything out there!
teo-jia-xin.notable_achievements:
Champions of Singapore Youth Policy Competition 2025
Champions of Singapore Undergraduate Policy Competition 2024
Merit Award in Engineering Innovation Challenge 2023 and 2025

ean-cheng.name: Ean Cheng
ean-cheng.writeup: Hi Ean here! A Y3 Material Science Student...idk what I'm supposed to write here HAHA

ian-tay-rongde.name: Ian Tay Rongde
ian-tay-rongde.masters: M.Sc. in Semiconductor Technology and Operations
ian-tay-rongde.writeup: Current student in the MSc Semiconductor Technology and Operations programme. BEng Materials Science and Engineering graduate with a minor in statistics.
ian-tay-rongde.notable_achievements: Lee Kuan Yew Gold Medal, IES Gold Medal (Materials Science and Engineering), two-time MSE Prize award-winner.
ian-tay-rongde.interests_hobbies: Drawing, learning more about the world.

karishma-thirumaran.name: Karishma Thirumaran
karishma-thirumaran.writeup:
Hi, I’m Karishma! I’m a Y3 undergraduate majoring in Computer Engineering.

Currently, I am interested in innovation in software engineering, and exploring the diverse opportunities in the sector such as automation, app development and AI. I am also passionate about business and entrepreneurship, and was part of the Jan'24 NOC Munich batch.

Other than watching bad hallmark christmas movies, my personal interests include playing the drums and english literature. Feel free to connect with me to discuss about anything from cs to seasonal movies!

yeo-meng-han.name: Yeo Meng Han
yeo-meng-han.writeup: Hi! I'm an undergraduate at the National University of Singapore (NUS) studying computer engineering under the Engineering Scholars (E-Scholars) Programme. I'm very passionate about entrepreneurship and developments in the field of technology and innovation - specifically the forefront of Robotics, Computer Vision and AI. At the moment, I'm deeply interested in manifesting the power of LLM via AI Agents and Retrieval-Augmented-Generation (RAG).
yeo-meng-han.notable_achievements:
NUS Computing Lifehack Project Director 2023
AI Singapore National AI Challenge Track 3 Top 3 Winner
NOC Alumni (NOC Vietnam AY23/24 Semester 2)
yeo-meng-han.interests_hobbies:
Watching documentaries
Strategy Computer Games
Manga / Korean manhwa!
yeo-meng-han.masters: M.Sc. in Data Science & Machine Learning

ryan-tan.name: Ryan Tan
ryan-tan.writeup: Hi, I'm Ryan, majoring in Computer Engineering. I am interested in software development and exploring AI/ML tools. Aside from academics, I also like playing table tennis and long distance running.
ryan-tan.interests_hobbies:
Long distance running
Table Tennis
Dance

shan-yu-xuan.name: Shan Yu Xuan
shan-yu-xuan.writeup: Hi! I am Yu Xuan, I had a lot of fun during my NUS journey, going to winter school, exchange, overseas trips and more. I will be going to Silicon Valley for internship soon.

man-juncheng.name: Man Juncheng
man-juncheng.writeup: Hey there! I'm currently pursuing graduate studies under the E-Scholars programme, enrolled in the MSc. Computer Engineering programme, with a specialisation in Computing Hardware Infrastructure! I am interested in the fields of computing hardware and hardware-software integration, having completed projects on embedded systems, FPGA accelerators, and quantum computing hardware. Through my exposures in the United States, I currently see great potential in the field of quantum computing, and intend to pursue further studies in the design and optimisation of quantum hardware to realise fault-tolerant quantum computing. Let us embrace the future :)
man-juncheng.notable_achievements:
Intel Cup Undergraduate Electronic Design Contest – Embedded System Design Invitational Contest 2024 First Prize Winner
AI Singapore National AI Challenge 2024 Track 3 Top 3 Winner
Dean's List in all Eligible Undergraduate Semesters
man-juncheng.interests_hobbies:
Fishing, specialisation in Ajing style
Fine Arts and Graphic Design
Hiking and Outdoor Exploration

zhuo-jiaying.name: Zhuo Jiaying
zhuo-jiaying.writeup: Hi! I'm Zhuo Jiaying, a year 3 chemical engineering student. I took SUSEP in year 2 sem 2 and am doing a specialisation in process engineering! 🥳

kelvyna-lee.name: Kelvyna Lee
kelvyna-lee.writeup:
Loving. Responsible. Respectful. These are words that people use to describe me. My values are shaped by my Christian faith and by my close-knit family.

I am thankful that I have been awarded the NUS Engineering Scholarship. I have also been offered a conditional admission to the Duke-NUS NUS Engineering and Medicine Track programme.

Currently, I play Lawn Bowls for Team Singapore. This is a sport which requires mental strength and focus to bowl consistently, and the ability to adjust quickly to different in-game scenarios.

I also serve regularly in church through singing and serving on the Audio-Visual team. My passion for missions has led me to Cambodia to bring joy to the less privileged, and I look forward to serving God’s people wherever I am called to!

goh-ting-en-jonathan.name: Goh Ting En Jonathan
goh-ting-en-jonathan.writeup: Hello there! I’m Jonathan, currently enrolled in the Master of Science in Semiconductor Technology and Operations, and a chemical engineering graduate. I’m very passionate about semiconductors and how engineering can integrate multiple scientific concepts to produce cutting edge technologies that help our everyday lives. On a more personal note, I enjoy climbing mountains and nature trails (amateur level but must have some challenge of course hehe) as well as anything basketball related! While that may sound healthy, it is definitely evened out with my love for food whoops :)
goh-ting-en-jonathan.notable_achievements:
Overseas VIA with TSMC in Taiwan (AY2024/2025 Special Term)
SEP (University of Texas at Austin AY2023/2024 Semester 2)
Basketball coaching assistant for alma mater Dunman High School (2023 to present)
goh-ting-en-jonathan.interests_hobbies:
Basketball
Fishing
Nerdy stuff (Star Wars, LOTR, Transformers etc)
goh-ting-en-jonathan.masters: M.Sc. in Semiconductor Technology and Operations

muhammad-fariz-bin-mohammad-yusri.name: Muhammad Fariz Bin Mohammad Yusri
muhammad-fariz-bin-mohammad-yusri.writeup: Hey everyone, my name is Fariz Yusri. I was previously from Chemical Engineering, and decided to expand my knowledge towards Business Analytics. I love playing doing sports on my free time (e.g football, running, lifting) and also photography/videography!

nga-jing-wen.name: Nga Jing Wen
nga-jing-wen.writeup: hi, i spend a lot of time coiling audio cables and doing math!

lock-de-ying-ethan.name: Lock De Ying Ethan
lock-de-ying-ethan.writeup: Hi! I'm Ethan, a Year 3 Mechanical Engineering student in the Engineering Scholars Programme! Currently, I'm majoring in Mechanical Engineering, with a specialisation in Robotics. My current academic plans are to embark on the M.Sc. in Electronic Engineering with a view to enter the Semiconductor industry. Come talk to me if you're interested in joining or already part of the local semiconductor scene! :D

gerianne-lee-shyi-chee.name: Gerianne Lee Shyi Chee
gerianne-lee-shyi-chee.writeup:
Hello! My name is Gerianne Lee and I am a Y2 Mechanical Engineering student (with a specialisation in Energy and Sustainability and a minor in Geosciences)

I am always looking to explore new opportunities and meet new people, so feel free to connect with me anytime! :p

nicholas-chia-yik-kiat.name: Nicholas Chia Yik Kiat
nicholas-chia-yik-kiat.writeup: Hi I am Nicholas, a Y3 Mechanical Engineering student specialising in Aeronautical Engineering. I love cats and I am interested in anything aviation related!
nicholas-chia-yik-kiat.notable_achievements:
(AY24/25 Sem 1) Purdue University Dean’s List, Semester Honors
(AY24/25 Sem 2) NUS Mechanical Engineering Dean’s List

amelia-ong.name: Amelia Ong
amelia-ong.writeup: Hi, I’m Amelia! I’m passionate about MedTech and love exploring new innovations in the field. I’m a bubbly person who’s always up for a chat, so feel free to approach me anytime!

savannah-chan.name: Savannah Chan
savannah-chan.writeup: i do muay thai & music in my free time :-)

lee-yu-han.name: Lee Yu Han
lee-yu-han.writeup: Hi, I'm Yu Han, Y3 BME, with Life Science second major! I'm interested in biomaterials and tissue engineering and currently undertaking a UROP under Prof Liu Yuxin's lab.

ryan-leong.name: Ryan Leong
ryan-leong.writeup:
Hi I’m Ryan Leong and I am a Y2 Computer Engineering student under the E-Scholars program staying in RC4. I will be going for NOC SV in the year of 2025 :)

Outside of academics, I am interested in playing guitar and performing, I am a member of RC4ME and planning to join NUS Amplified. Additionally, I like exercising and playing sports like football.

jin-zihan.name: Jin Zihan
jin-zihan.writeup: Hello! I am Zihan, a Year 3 Computer Engineering student with a specialisation in Advanced Electronics. In my free time I enjoy listening to music and reading :)

che-luyang.name: Che Luyang
che-luyang.writeup: Hello there! I'm Luyang. I'm currently a final year E-Scholar studying computer engineering, and I'm also currently doing the Advanced Electronics specialisation. I have a strong life-long interest for tinkering and building things, be it with electronics or other mediums like wood and leather. I have a love-hate relationship with the outdoors, in which I love cycling around nature, going hiking, and taking casual walks with loved ones, but I also hate mosquitoes, getting blisters, and extremely humid weather.
che-luyang.notable_achievements:
Learnt depth-first search algorithm on the spot during the middle of a programming exam
Top Student Award for CG2271, AY2024/2025 Semester 1
Survived descending an abandoned Malaysian mountain trail alone at night

bryan-loh.name: Bryan Loh
bryan-loh.writeup: Hi! I’m Bryan, a Y2 undergraduate majoring in Chemical Engineering.

bryan-chua.name: Bryan Chua
bryan-chua.writeup:
Hi! I am Bryan, a Mechanical Engineering student under the E-Scholars Programme. I am also currently a resident in College of Alice and Peter Tan. During my studies in NUS, I am interested in pursuing a minor in entrepreneurship, as well as joining a NOC programme. Outside of my academics, I am excited to explore some of my interests, such as picking up tennis and learning how to bake!

I am thrilled to be part of the E-Scholars community and I believe the academic rigour of the programme, as well as the opportunities it presents will allow me to realize my full potential and discover more about myself along the way!

jonathan-chew.name: Jonathan Chew
jonathan-chew.writeup:
I an Jonathan! I am a Y1 mech eng student under the E-scholars programme and I’m currently in Tembusu.
I’m planning to a double major in computing!

joshua-er.name: Joshua Er
joshua-er.writeup: Hi Im Josh doing Mechanical Engineering. I have lived overseas for 13 years of my life studying in Shanghai. When I came back to Singapore I finished IB in Tanglin Trust School before proceeding to national service. Besides academics, I do occasionally enjoy running and exploring new areas so if you ever have a good route, feel free to contact me.

ngan-ka-fye.name: Ngan Ka Fye
ngan-ka-fye.writeup:
Hello! I’m Dean, a Mechanical Engineering major minoring in Psychology.

Wait, Engineering and Psychology? Yes, I take an interest in a broad range of subjects, and I am fortunate to be able to further my intellectual development with NUS College.

I love keeping active in my free time, whether it be swimming, cycling, running, climbing, hiking, badminton, table tennis… yeah I really do have many interests.
ngan-ka-fye.notable_achievements:
• Slept at least 8 hours a day on average throughout secondary school.
• Slept at least 7 hours a day on average throughout junior college.
• Currently sleep debt-free.

kae-lim.name: Kae Lim
kae-lim.writeup: I'm doing Mechanical Engineering but I also have interests in material science, chemistry and linguistics. I enjoy working with my hands, including woodcarving, building furniture and legos.

laura-lee.name: Laura Lee
laura-lee.writeup:
As an Engineering Scholar specializing in Mechanical Engineering with a focus on Energy and Sustainability, I am committed to addressing global challenges, particularly those related to climate change and environmental sustainability. I aspire to attain the skills and knowledge necessary to innovate and develop transformative solutions in the energy sector.

With a deep passion for mathematics, physics, and engineering, I am driven to apply my expertise to create sustainable technologies that contribute to a better future. My approach combines analytical rigor with a commitment to social responsibility, recognizing the importance of leveraging my opportunities to effect positive change.

I am dedicated to continuous learning and collaboration, believing that true innovation arises from the intersection of diverse ideas and perspectives. Through my work, I aim to contribute meaningfully to the development of sustainable solutions that will shape a resilient and equitable world for future generations.

loh-ming-cheng.name: Loh Ming Cheng
loh-ming-cheng.writeup: Hello! I am ming cheng, staying at tembusu college. I am studying Mechanical Engineering, hoping to do a second major in computer science too!
loh-ming-cheng.interests_hobbies: Sports, Playing board games and Nature!

mao-ruijie.name: Mao Ruijie
mao-ruijie.writeup:
Hi! I'm Ruijie, Mechanical Engineering undergraduate in NUS. I am grateful to be part of the Engineering Scholars Programme and its community of passionate individuals.

I constantly seek to learn and gain a deeper understanding of the world around me, from the socio-political dynamics of society to the physics of the universe. At the moment, I aim to pursue computing in conjunction with engineering to better leverage the strengths of our digital systems as a tool to understand the physical world. Examples include computational fluid dynamics, finite element analysis, and computational astrophysics (and numerical relativity).

I am also invested in a wide array of hobbies in my free time, such as photography, graphic design, video editing, and playing the piano. Though, I am completely self-taught in all these areas.

If you have any questions about me, feel free to contact me!
mao-ruijie.notable_achievements:
Student Leader of the Year 2021 (River Valley High School)
Chinese Calligraphy National Youth Competition Champion

tan-ser-gi-chloe.name: Tan Ser Gi Chloe
tan-ser-gi-chloe.writeup: Hello I am chloe. I study Mechanical Engineering under the e scholars program. I reside in RC4 and im from Noctua. My academic plan as of now is to have an aeronautical engineering specialisation, and hopefully, to be able to complete a second major in economics. I hope to be able to go for a student exchange program (SEP) in Finland, to experience what it is like to live and study in the world’s happiest country! Apart from academics, i find joy in road cycling, and I hope to be able to cycle from Singapore to China one day. I also enjoy playing weiqi, as it allows me to meet many different people from all walks of lives.
tan-ser-gi-chloe.notable_achievements:
2022-2023 Deans list.
China ASEAN board and card games international invitational tournament 2019.

lim-wei-en-wayne.name: Lim Wei En, Wayne
lim-wei-en-wayne.writeup: I am someone who is not very ambitious, but wishes to complete his degree in 3 years as well as a Masters by the fourth year. Being someone who over-committed himself to leadership positions in JC and secondary school, I wish to use university to pace myself, and understand more about the field I am going into, rather than dive into student life planning and coordination. Apart from academics, I wish to participate in dance, badminton and volleyball on the RC level, inter-RC and inter-house level games as well as fly drone.
lim-wei-en-wayne.notable_achievements:
EAGLES award (2021)
NPCC Long Service Award (2021)
6th in Army Intelligence Officer Cadet Course (2022)
lim-wei-en-wayne.interests_hobbies:
Playing sports
Dancing
Listening to music

luo-xin-yue.name: Luo Xin Yue
luo-xin-yue.writeup: Hi, I'm Xin Yue. SCGS and RI alumnus. I go with the flow and idk what I'm doing half the time. You can't control what life throws at you most of the time so I don't worry about it too much.
luo-xin-yue.notable_achievements:
ESIS recipient (2022-2023)
getting the E-scholars scholarship

lee-yu-wei-ryan.name: Lee Yu Wei, Ryan
lee-yu-wei-ryan.writeup: I'm Ryan, an aspiring engineer looking to make a difference in the things I do. I'm constantly broadening my horizons, learning and experiencing new things every day and finding opportunities to put my knowledge to the test. Although I work hard, I also take the time to engage in a wide range of hobbies, keeping my mind refreshed yet active.
lee-yu-wei-ryan.notable_achievements: Research paper on Feasibility of Superman Memory

jennifer-nah-su-en.name: Jennifer Nah Su En
jennifer-nah-su-en.writeup: Hello! I'm Jennifer, a Mechanical Engineering student under the E-Scholars Programme. I stay in Ridge View Residential College and wish to pursue a Specialisation in Energy and Sustainability to deepen both my knowledge and passion for environmental causes. I also intend on taking up a Minor in Forensic Science to broaden my horizons!

ashlee-chang.name: Ashlee Chang
ashlee-chang.writeup: Hi! My name is Ashlee and I’m majoring in Electrical Engineering. I’m passionate about Robotics and participated in competitions like Robocup and WRO for since P6! I hope to learn more about robotics and electronics in NUS and look forward to my time here :D

then-chin-kiat.name: Then Chin Kiat
then-chin-kiat.writeup:
Hi, I’m Chin Kiat, a first year freshie and will be studying Electrical Engineering.

I’m interested in electronic devices and have interest in consumer audio equipment such as headphones, in-ear monitors, dacs, etc. I’m also an avid enjoyer of Japanese music, anime and manga.

I’m glad and humbled to be part of the E-scholars programme and plan to make full use of my time here.

grace-lau.name: Grace Lau
grace-lau.writeup:
Hi! I'm Grace, an Electrical Engineering major at NUS CDE! In my 4 years at NUS, I'm excited to learn how to apply my theoretical knowledge to ideate solutions to real life problems, which I hope to achieve by taking the 2nd major in iDP.

I look forward to making full use of the valuable opportunities in NUS to deepen my theoretical knowledge.

Feel free to contact me regarding any enquiries or project ideas!

yap-han.name: Yap Han
yap-han.writeup: Hello! I am Yap Han, an Electrical Engineering Student under the E-Scholars Programme. I am aiming for a career involving sustainable technologies, in particular, clean power generation and its implementation in Singapore.

yap-jit-en-faith.name: Yap Jit En Faith
yap-jit-en-faith.writeup: Hi, I’m Faith, a Year 2 Electrical Engineering student specializing in Space Technology. I’m passionate about exploring the world of electromagnetism and its applications. In the coming years, I hope to gain firsthand experience in the industry and understand the real-world implementations of electromagnetism.

zheng-weilin.name: Zheng Weilin
zheng-weilin.writeup: Hi I'm Weilin! A fun fact about me is that I prefer popsicles to ice cream cones🥹 and my pet peeve is the smell of smoking😬. I’m very interested in the bonding and welfare committee because I really want E-scholars to have more opportunities to bond with one another on a regular basis!! This builds a support system that E-scholars can rely on, especially since we are all majoring in engineering, and so we can relate with one another better and understand each other’s stress and concerns( for eg on a common engineering course). Being more familiar with one another also prevents E-scholars from feeling paiseh in seeking help from other E-scholars. 😂 Also, I understand that most E-scholars stay in RC4. For me, I stay in nusc but the friends I have made from the E-scholars orientation stay in RC4 so I’m really sad that there’s little opportunities for me to interact with them :(( Hence, I really want to join the welfare committee to help organise bonding events so that E-scholars can have many opportunities to bond with one another!! :))) Apart from this reason, I also want to be involved in preparing welfare packs so that I can help E-scholars feel less stressed and more encouraged during exam period. All in all, the first few friends I have made in NUS are from the community of E-scholars so I really feel strongly for giving back and contributing to the welfare of E-scholars. :))
zheng-weilin.notable_achievements:
When I was in junior college, I was a subject representative in class. I helped to gather feedback from my classmates ( for example, did they find the mode of teaching by our teacher effective/ did they find the homework given by our teacher too easy) and shared them with my teacher. I also helped to organise a class gathering whereby my class and our teacher came together to catch up and have fun.:)

While I was in my CCA- chinese orchestra (CO), I volunteered to be an orientation group leader during a musical exchange between my junior college’s CO and 2 other schools’ CO. I helped to organise ice breaker games to facilitate bonding among students from different schools in my group. At the end of the day, I was glad that the students in my group got to know one another better and had fun playing the games together. :D
zheng-weilin.interests_hobbies: I enjoy spending time with my friends, catching up on each other’s lives and understanding their current thoughts. Sometimes, I enjoy folding origami (eg hearts) according to YouTube videos and giving them to people I know :))) because I enjoy making them happy! Other times, I enjoy swimming/ jogging in a park.

janelle-tang.name: Janelle Tang
janelle-tang.writeup:
Hello! I am Janelle, a Year 1 student majoring in Biomedical Engineering under the Engineering Scholars Programme at NUS.

While I am particularly interested in the area of research and tissue engineering, I am open to exploring other areas of specialisation and I hope to find something that I will enjoy doing in the future.

I am grateful for this opportunity and I hope that this experience will help me grow both academically and as a person!

tan-xin-yi.name: Tan Xin Yi
tan-xin-yi.writeup:
Hello, I am Xin Yi, an undergraduate student majoring in Biomedical Engineering.  I am particularly interested in the manufacturing and innovation of biomedical devices. This encourages me to specialize in biomedical materials. It fascinates me  how engineers can invent new machines to reduce patients' discomfort, or provide alternatives to invasive surgical methods. After graduation, I aspire to contribute to society through my work in engineering. As such, I am grateful for the many opportunities provided by the E-Scholar community and look forward to the next few years of my academic journey.

Aside from my academics, I also find solace in reading. I love the feeling of being transported into starkly different situations and experiencing them from a new perspective. Often, you can find me hunched over and immersed in a book. I also enjoy spending time with my family and friends, whether it's cycling at East Coast Park or huddling together over a game of Mahjong.
tan-xin-yi.notable_achievements:
Vice-President of AVA Club
Best Improvement Award in J1 Promotional Examination
Honour Roll for JC2 Common Test

lin-hong-yi.name: Lin Hong Yi
lin-hong-yi.writeup:
HHI! My name is Lin Hong Yi, and I am a Engineering Science student under the Engineering Scholars Programme. I am currently living in Residential College 4! I am a Tennis addict and train regularly =). I also have side hustles in other sports such as Floorball and Volleyball!!! Hit me up for a sesh anytime, I'm sure they'll be time.

Academically, I am also pursing a second major in iDP as I love problem solving and design! I hope to secure a Semestral SEP in the upcomming academic year as well as a year long NOC after! At the same time I'm also exploring opportunities in Robotics as well as med-tech.

amberly-pock-xin-yi.name: Amberly Pock Xin Yi
amberly-pock-xin-yi.writeup:
Hi! I'm Amberly, a Year 1 Environmental and Sustainability Engineering Student with a second major in Sustainable Urban Development, and I live at Residential College 4! I chose Environmental and Sustainability Engineering as I'm passionate about sustainability and want to learn how to create or improve on solutions that makes the world a better place to live in, for both present and future generations. Areas I'm particularly interested in are renewable energy, and circular economy.

Currently, I'm actively participating in NUS CCAs such as d'Hoppers, SAVE and Project OceaNUS. In my time at NUS, I hope to take up various research opportunities and internships to put what I've learnt to practical use, and embark on projects that can help the environment and those around me!

hannah-hoo.name: Hannah Hoo
hannah-hoo.writeup: Hello! My name is Hannah and I'm a Y1 Environmental and Sustainability Engineering student, with a second major in Sustainable Urban Development. I am currently staying at College of Alice and Peter Tan. Feel free to find me to chat about anything!

ong-choon-heng.name: Ong Choon Heng
ong-choon-heng.writeup:
Hi! I am Choon Heng, currently studying Industrial System Engineering under the E-Scholars Programme. I am staying in U-Town at Residential College 4.

I hope to be able to delve deeper into my major with a specialisation in Supply Chain Analytics, while also meeting new people along the way.

Beyond academics, I also enjoy travelling to overseas and eating good food!

teo-jun-jie-jeremy.name: Teo Jun Jie Jeremy
teo-jun-jie-jeremy.writeup: Hello! I am Jeremy and I am currently majoring in ISE.
teo-jun-jie-jeremy.interests_hobbies:

Enjoys music and playing musical instruments

hazel-yeo.name: Hazel Yeo
hazel-yeo.writeup:
Hello! My name is Hazel Yeo and I am a Y1 Materials Science and Engineering (MSE) student living in RC4. I chose MSE as this major marries my interests in both physics and chemistry.

During my study at NUS, I hope to go on exchange under the Student Exchange Programme (SEP) to broaden my world view and equip myself with essential interpersonal skills.

In my free time, I like to try out new food places and cycle around Singapore! I also enjoy volunteering at nearby community centres and interacting with my immediate community.

Hello!! My name is Kayla Khoo and I am a Y1 Materials Science and Engineering student under the Engineering Scholars Programme. I am currently staying at CAPT!

I am currently planning on taking a second major in Innovation & Design. For my major, I do not have any specific area of interest yet but hope to discover one soon further into the academic year!

Outside of academics, I enjoy dancing (different genres of dance!) and performing together with my friends! I also like to destress through art and crafts during my free time and going for walks outside. :)

chin-yi-en.name: Chin Yi En
chin-yi-en.writeup: Hi! I’m Yi En, a year 2 Materials Science and Engineering student under the E-scholars programme. I’m currently doing a second major in Innovation and Design as I hope to develop people-centric design skills to design relevant solutions to real world problems as an engineer. Though I currently intend on pursuing research in the future, I’m open to opportunities in other fields to grow my horizons.  Looking forward to the experiences and opportunities ahead!

I do prefer the software side of computer engineering slightly more than the electrical side, but I do appreciate a healthy mix of both best, as I find that it excites me and pushes me to keep learning the most.

I hope to enhance my coding and design skills through projects, internships and overseas attachments. I'm interested to explore more about artificial intelligence, robotics and other programming languages. As someone with a keen interest in aviation, I am excited to see how I can apply what I have learnt to make air travel safer and more efficient.

I really enjoy travelling, learning about new places and cultures, and exploring different cuisines. But chicken rice will always have a special place in my heart!

Hi I am Ryan, a Y1 CEG student looking into studying a specialisation (haven't decided on one yet). However, I do know that I am passionate in both hardware and software and bridging the two together.

heng-teng-yi.name: Heng Teng Yi
heng-teng-yi.writeup:
Hi! I am Teng Yi, a Y1 Computer Engineering student under the E-Scholars Programme. I enjoy designing, fabricating, wiring and programming robots (and other electronics projects).

I got my start with electronics building autonomous soccer robots for RoboCup Junior in 2019, and I've been eager to learn more about electronics design ever since.

In NUS, I'm hoping to delve more into integrated circuit design, processor design and low-level firmware programming; the area where hardware and software intersect is where I believe I work best.

Outside of class, I maintain software libraries and design circuit boards and casings for my educational robotics product line EVN. Apart from that, I intend to join the Bumblebee CCA to continue honing my electrical engineering and system integration skills.

In my free time, I like playing Ultimate Frisbee and spending time with my cats (they're very cute).

Technical Lead (& Co-Creator) of EVN Alpha, a robot controller for education & competition

arthur-ong-zheng-jie.name: Arthur Ong Zheng Jie
arthur-ong-zheng-jie.writeup: Hello! I'm Arthur, studying Mechanical Engineering in NUS. My interests lie in robotics and automation, aspiring to create technologies that improve our quality of life. As an E-Scholar, I'm thankful for its community of knowledgeable and passionate people. By engaging with like-minded peers and industry professionals, I hope to gain insights that will enable me to transform my interests into impactful solutions.

elena-ng-sze-hwee.name: Elena Ng Sze Hwee
elena-ng-sze-hwee.writeup: Hii, I'm Elena Ng, a Mechanical Engineering student at NUS. I am deeply honoured to be part of the E-Scholars Programme here in NUS, and am excited for the opportunities in store for me. During my time in NUS, I hope to develop holistically by giving back to my community through my time in the College of Alice and Peter Tan, and expand my knowledge as a mechanical engineer with a specialisation in Energy and Sustainability, with a Minor in Mathematics.

lin-jing-yang.name: Lin Jing Yang
lin-jing-yang.writeup: Hello! I am Jing Yang, studying Mechanical Engineering. My sport interests include a mix of swimming, cycling, running and kayaking. I also like to nerd out on math and engineering concepts. My favourite movie is Interstellar and I write stories sometimes. I am passionate in sustainability related technologies and am looking at a career relating to sustainability and aviation.

cheng-zhi-en-aaron.name: Cheng Zhi-En Aaron

Beyond academics, I am an avid runner who enjoys sports and embraces an active lifestyle. I also aspire to travel the world, exploring diverse cultures and learning more about the world around us.

lee-jun-feng.name: Lee Jun Feng
lee-jun-feng.writeup: Hello! I’m Jun Feng, a Year 1 Electrical Engineering student currently staying at the College of Alice & Peter Tan. I’m excited for a fun and fulfilling university journey ahead! I’m curious about just about anything—from deep conversations to light-hearted chats—so feel free to reach out. I’m always happy to connect, listen, and share. I also enjoy playing sports for fun whenever I get the chance!

chloe-ong-su-neng.name: Chloe Ong Su Neng
chloe-ong-su-neng.writeup: Hi! I'm Chloe Ong and I study Electrical Engineering under the E-Scholars Programme at NUS! Looking forward to my time here :c)

choy-keanric.name: Choy Keanric
choy-keanric.writeup: Hello! I'm Keanric, a first-year Electrical Engineering Student that is also pursuing a minor in Biomedical Engineering. I'm interested in circuit analysis and finding out how different gadgets work. Looking forward to my time in NUS!

leo-kee-kiat-ethan.name: Leo Kee Kiat, Ethan
leo-kee-kiat-ethan.writeup:
Hi everyone! I am Ethan and I am a Year 1 undergraduate in Biomedical Engineering in the Engineering Scholars Programme. I hope to be able to create healthcare solutions that will be life-changing for the patients that use it, so I intend to participate in Ideathons where I can make my ideas come to life. I am also looking to deepen my skills by pursuing a second degree in either mechanical or electrical engineering but am currently undecided. 

In my free time, enjoy tinkering with CAD and playing the guitar. I also love learning new languages. The picture above was taken when I went for a language exchange to Spain! Feel free to reach out to talk about anything! :D

tan-jing-ren-sarah.name: Tan Jing Ren, Sarah
tan-jing-ren-sarah.writeup:
Nice to meet you, I'm Sarah, a Year 1 E-Scholar majoring in Biomedical Engineering. Since young, I found myself inclined towards the sciences and maths, while also being a creative and hands-on learner. Choosing Engineering was a no-brainer as it perfectly integrates these different strengths, but what drew me towards Biomedical Engineering was its potential for growth and innovation in the years to come. Not only that, it also opens opportunities to work towards the meaningful purpose of improving the quality of life and health of those around me, and potentially the world at large. I work best in environments where I can combine problem-solving skills with human-centric creativity, and I believe that Biomedical Engineering will give me that platform to design and refine technologies that are not only scientifically sound but also address problems empathetically. 

In the long term, I see myself as a leader in the field—someone who steps up and takes charge of revolutionary projects, such as developing biocompatible devices that aid bodily functions or AI-assisted diagnostics. I am particularly intrigued by the intersection of materials science and biology, where engineered biomaterials could mimic biological functions or promote self-healing in damaged tissues. To achieve these goals and deepen my understanding of this field, I plan to specialise in Biomedical materials, but I am also open to exploring any other aspect of Biomedical Engineering. 

People around me would describe me as an extrovert with a never-ending flow of energy, and I love a good conversation! I am constantly seeking for opportunities to learn and glean new insights from others, or to share some experiences of my own. Feel free to connect with me if you have any questions, or just need someone to talk to!
tan-jing-ren-sarah.notable_achievements:
MOE Engineering and Tech Programme Scholar
House Vice-Captain in Eunoia Junior College
Most Innovative Project Award (Young Defence Scientists Programme by DSTA)
tan-jing-ren-sarah.interests_hobbies:
Sports (Especially Badminton, Tchoukball and Ultimate Frisbee!)
Any activity that involves socialising (Emceeing, Volunteering, Music etc)
Tinkering and trying new things

laris-tan-shi-xuan.name: Laris Tan Shi Xuan
laris-tan-shi-xuan.writeup: Hi! I am Laris, a Year 1 student majoring in Industrial and Systems Engineering with a second major in Data Analytics. In the future, I hope to create a more efficient workplace environment and to ensure the reliability of the systems I will be handling :)

lee-ming-yi.writeup:
Listen • Master • Yield.
Hello! I am an Engineering Scholar keen to explore the application of the industrial and systems engineering principles of optimisation and simulation in various industries, tapping on technical skills in problem solving and analysis that integrate with softer skills in management or project coordination, as well as my experiences spanning the public, private, and people sectors.
As a hobby or so to speak, I enjoy reading on and exploring how spaces and places have developed over time in Singapore and worldwide. Other than my sight, my hearing is satisfied by listening to music, particularly (English) pop, although I am starting to realise that many acts familiar to me are rock.

siddharth-s-arumuganainar.name: Siddharth S Arumuganainar
siddharth-s-arumuganainar.writeup: Hi! I'm Sid, a freshie from CEG, though I'm more interested in software than hardware. I am especially intrigued in learning about and implementing AI. In my free time I enjoy cycling, playing basketball and binging netflix.
siddharth-s-arumuganainar.notable_achievements:
1. I am an escholar :)
2. Best Computer Vision Award at Determined AI Hackathon 2024
3. SSEF 2021 Silver & Yale-NUS Special Award

anjana-imadith-dissanayake.name: Anjana Imadith Dissanayake
anjana-imadith-dissanayake.writeup: I am a cheery individual who enjoys the company of my friends and loved ones around me. I strive to achieve my goals to the best of my ability while interacting effectively with those around me. I believe my academic strength lies in learning to understand concepts  completely rather than in order to fulfill the requirements of an exam or curriculum. I believe mastering this will allow me to have a balanced life between academics and extracurricular activities, and allow me to make the most use of the opportunities given to me as an E-Scholar
anjana-imadith-dissanayake.notable_achievements:
Academic achievements in school
Participating in both sports and performing arts CCAs in school
Improving fitness during my time in National Service

jay-tan-jun-jie.name: Jay Tan Jun Jie
jay-tan-jun-jie.writeup: Jay's passion lies in the marine litter issue, devoting considerable time into educating youths in Singapore on it. This and the wider realm of sustainability forms the drive for Jay to pursue Chemical Engineering. He believes that through such a far reaching and in depth programme, he hopes to play an  important part in the fight for sustainability.
jay-tan-jun-jie.notable_achievements:
Was a member of the Climate Youth Development Programme 2025
Was a member of the YSEALI Blue Economy Regional Workshop
Founder and Director of Save The Wave
jay-tan-jun-jie.interests_hobbies:
Facilitating Sustainability Workshops
Skilled Handicrafts (e.g. Sewing)
Learning New Things

cheong-zhi-yong-timeus.name: Cheong Zhi Yong Timeus
cheong-zhi-yong-timeus.writeup: Hi! My name is Timeus, and I have always enjoyed learning more about our world, regardless of whichever field it may be! Geography, History, Math, Science are all things I love, and I hope to be able to join academia and the field of research in the future!
cheong-zhi-yong-timeus.notable_achievements:
NUS Geography Challenge 2019 Top Performing Individual (Gold) & 2021 Geography National Team
ACJC Swim Team (2021-2022) member and 2022 Inter-School Games 200m and 400m Freestyle finalist
Holy Innocents' High School Student Leader Board Executive Community member, Vice-Head of Student Activity Council.
cheong-zhi-yong-timeus.interests_hobbies:
I have an interest in learning about all sorts of things, and I enjoy reading textbooks to find out more about other topics.
I also have an interest in playing the guitar! I plan on joining (and hopefully at this moment, am in) RVRC's Jukebox Interest Group.
I enjoy going for hikes, especially overseas!

ryan-wee-khai-boon.name: Ryan Wee Khai Boon
ryan-wee-khai-boon.writeup:
Hello! I am Ryan, a first-year Chemical Engineering student, currently residing at Residential College 4 under the E-Scholars Programme. My primary interest lies in exploring how green energy technologies can be scaled sustainably to meet the world’s growing needs, with a particular focus on supporting Singapore’s net-zero targets by 2050. To deepen my expertise, I plan to pursue a minor in Hydrogen and Low Carbon Technologies alongside a second major in Data Analytics, equipping myself with both the technical knowledge and analytical skills needed for informed, data-driven decision-making in this field.

Beyond my core discipline, I am passionate about finance, especially the analytical frameworks investors use to evaluate and value businesses. In my free time, I enjoy playing football and golf.
ryan-wee-khai-boon.notable_achievements:
3rd Place, NUS-SDS Datathon 2025
NSF of the Year 2024

bong-shi-qing-sarah.name: Bong Shi Qing Sarah
bong-shi-qing-sarah.writeup: I’m an introvert at first, but once we’re close, my inner crazy extrovert comes out. I love tackling new challenges, working on hands-on projects and research, and I’m always eager to learn something new!
bong-shi-qing-sarah.notable_achievements:
Ngee Ann Scholarship
Croda Singapore Gold Medal and Prize

sudeeksha-padmanaban.name: Sudeeksha Padmanaban
sudeeksha-padmanaban.writeup: I’m passionate about pharmaceuticals and food sectors within chemical engineering. I like trying new things to step out of my comfort zone and gain new experiences—because life’s all about exploring and learning. Outside class, I enjoy chatting with friends and, I love to travel and try new food options! I am really grateful for this opportunity and hope to make full use of it :)
sudeeksha-padmanaban.notable_achievements:
Secretary in Greenlink CCA at NJC
SINDA Excellence Award for Integrated Programme

loh-zi-quan-keith.name: Loh Zi Quan Keith
loh-zi-quan-keith.writeup: Hi, I'm Keith, a year 1 Chemical Engineering undergraduate. I enrolled in engineering to learn how math and science can be applied to solve real world problems, and plan to do a second major in Mathematics. In my free time I like to run, cycle and spend time with my friends.
loh-zi-quan-keith.notable_achievements:
A*STAR Science Award (Junior College)
1st Place in Singapore Mathematical Society Essay Competition (2022)

senthilkumar-praveen.name: Senthilkumar Praveen
senthilkumar-praveen.writeup:
Hi! I’m Praveen and I’m majoring in Civil Engineering. I’m interested to learn how sustainability and digitalisation can shape the future of our built environment.
 I’m currently staying in RVRC and I love staying active by playing a bunch of sports, but mainly basketball.

kate-lovelace-layba-agpaoa.name: Kate Lovelace Layba Agpaoa
kate-lovelace-layba-agpaoa.writeup: Hi I'm Kate Lovelace, a year 1 Civil Engineering undergraduate under the Engineering Scholars Programme. I'm currently staying in RC4 and I'm from Draco!
kate-lovelace-layba-agpaoa.interests_hobbies: Playing the guitar, reading, listening to music :)

choong-k-ron.name: Choong K-Ron
choong-k-ron.writeup: Hellos! I'm K-Ron, a Y1 student majoring in Infrastructure & Project Management while with the E-Scholars Programme. I am particularly interested in the application of an engineering mindset/methodology in approaching real world and industry tasks and challenges. After graduation, I intend to pursue a career in the public service and am grateful for the opportunity to expand my horizons and my network through the programme, developing myself holistically as well as academically.
choong-k-ron.notable_achievements:
TP LEAD VaLEADictorian and Dual Medallist, Class of 2025
Harvard Prize Book (Singapore) 2024/25 Recipient
choong-k-ron.interests_hobbies:
Trying new things
Discussions and debate
Reading Webnovels

wong-shue-rou.name: Wong Shue Rou
wong-shue-rou.writeup: Hi y'all! I'm Shue Rou, and I'm a Y1 Industrial Design student under the Design Scholarship Program. As my major suggests, I love doing anything design or arts related in my free time. Throughout my time in NUS, I hope that I will be able to not only passionately contribute back to the community, but also learn more about myself from my peers!
wong-shue-rou.notable_achievements: Awarded the Humanities Scholarship in VJC (2023/2024)
